nodes.description: The initiating lesion is a heterozygous interstitial deletion of the 14q22-q23 region. Variable deletion breakpoints alter gene content, which explains why ocular, endocrine, craniofacial, limb, and neurodevelopmental manifestations vary across individuals.

evidence.reference_title: Anophthalmia, Global Developmental Delay, and Severe Dysphagia in a Young Girl With 14q22q23 Microdeletion Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: 14q22q23 microdeletion syndrome, also called Frias syndrome, is an extremely rare partial deletion of the long arm of chromosome 14 characterized by the anomalies of the pituitary gland, eyes, and hand/foot.

evidence.reference_title: Anophthalmia, Global Developmental Delay, and Severe Dysphagia in a Young Girl With 14q22q23 Microdeletion Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: This case report presents the case of a young child with a deletion in 14q22.2q23.1 region containing both BMP4 and OTX2 genes as well as sineoculis homeobox homolog 1 (SIX1) and sineoculis homeobox homolog 6 (SIX6) genes.

nodes.description: Decreased cerebral white matter volume has been reported in a substantial fraction of reviewed 14q22 microdeletion cases.

evidence.reference_title: Concurrent deletion of BMP4 and OTX2 genes, two master genes in ophthalmogenesis.
evidence.snippet: A review of twelve previously reported patients with 14q22 microdeletion revealed decreased white matter volume in half of the patients.

nodes.description: Anophthalmia represents the severe end of the congenital ocular malformation spectrum in Frias syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Concurrent deletion of BMP4 and OTX2 genes, two master genes in ophthalmogenesis.
evidence.snippet: Mutations of BMP4 and OTX2 often lead to eye defects, including anophthalmia-microphthalmia.

nodes.description: BMP4 dosage loss disrupts BMP/TGF-beta developmental signaling. This affects embryonic morphogenesis, eye development, skeletal development, and limb/digit patterning, contributing to ocular, craniofacial, and hand/foot findings.
nodes.id: BMP4 haploinsufficiency and BMP developmental signaling disruption

evidence.reference_title: Haploinsufficiency of BMP4 gene may be the underlying cause of Frías syndrome.
evidence.snippet: This gene family plays an important role during early embryogenesis, and the complex synergistic functions and redundancies of the BMPs led us to conclude that haploinsufficiency of BMP4 is likely to be responsible for the clinical expression of Frías syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Variable expressivity of syndromic BMP4-related eye, brain, and digital anomalies: A review of the literature and description of three new cases.
evidence.snippet: BMP4 has a number of roles in embryonic development including neurogenesis, lens induction, development of cartilage and bone, urogenital development, limb and digit patterning, hair follicle regeneration, as well as tooth formation.

nodes.description: Vermian hypoplasia broadens the brain-malformation spectrum of OTX2-region deletions.

evidence.reference_title: New insights into the phenotypic spectrum of 14q22q23 deletions: a case report and literature review.
evidence.snippet: In this report, we describe a case of large microdeletion encompassing OTX2 but not BMP4 presenting with a syndromic anophthalmia with corpus callosum hypoplasia, pituitary gland hypoplasia and vermian hypoplasia.

nodes.description: Choanal atresia is a reported craniofacial/airway malformation in a 14q22.3-q23.2 deletion case.

evidence.reference_title: Interstitial deletion 14q22.3-q23.2: genotype-phenotype correlation.
evidence.snippet: Here, we present a 12-year-old girl with dysmorphic face, choanal atresia, gastroesophageal reflux, and moderate developmental delay, in whom an interstitial deletion 14q22.3-q23.2 was detected using a 180k array comparative genome hybridization.

nodes.description: Corpus callosum hypoplasia can occur in OTX2-region 14q22q23 deletions.

evidence.reference_title: New insights into the phenotypic spectrum of 14q22q23 deletions: a case report and literature review.
evidence.snippet: In this report, we describe a case of large microdeletion encompassing OTX2 but not BMP4 presenting with a syndromic anophthalmia with corpus callosum hypoplasia, pituitary gland hypoplasia and vermian hypoplasia.

nodes.description: Gastroesophageal reflux has been reported in a child with a 14q22.3-q23.2 deletion.

evidence.reference_title: Interstitial deletion 14q22.3-q23.2: genotype-phenotype correlation.
evidence.snippet: Here, we present a 12-year-old girl with dysmorphic face, choanal atresia, gastroesophageal reflux, and moderate developmental delay, in whom an interstitial deletion 14q22.3-q23.2 was detected using a 180k array comparative genome hybridization.

nodes.description: Developmental delay is variable and can be moderate to severe depending on deletion content and multisystem involvement.

evidence.reference_title: Interstitial deletion 14q22.3-q23.2: genotype-phenotype correlation.
evidence.snippet: Here, we present a 12-year-old girl with dysmorphic face, choanal atresia, gastroesophageal reflux, and moderate developmental delay, in whom an interstitial deletion 14q22.3-q23.2 was detected using a 180k array comparative genome hybridization.

nodes.description: Intellectual disability is a common neurodevelopmental manifestation.

evidence.reference_title: Anophthalmia, Global Developmental Delay, and Severe Dysphagia in a Young Girl With 14q22q23 Microdeletion Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Intellectual disability and facial dysmorphism are other common manifestations.

nodes.description: Micrognathia or retrognathia can be detected prenatally and is part of the orofacial phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Haploinsufficiency of BMP4 and OTX2 in the Foetus with an abnormal facial profile detected in the first trimester of pregnancy.
evidence.snippet: Interstitial microdeletion 14q22q23 is a rare chromosomal syndrome associated with variable defects: microphthalmia/anophthalmia, pituitary anomalies, polydactyly/syndactyly of hands and feet, micrognathia/retrognathia.

nodes.description: Microphthalmia is a defining congenital ocular manifestation in the 14q22q23 deletion spectrum.

evidence.reference_title: Haploinsufficiency of BMP4 and OTX2 in the Foetus with an abnormal facial profile detected in the first trimester of pregnancy.
evidence.snippet: Interstitial microdeletion 14q22q23 is a rare chromosomal syndrome associated with variable defects: microphthalmia/anophthalmia, pituitary anomalies, polydactyly/syndactyly of hands and feet, micrognathia/retrognathia.

nodes.description: Supportive care is phenotype-directed across the multisystem presentation, including ophthalmologic, endocrine, neurodevelopmental, craniofacial, hand/foot, gastrointestinal, and airway manifestations when present.

evidence.reference_title: Anophthalmia, Global Developmental Delay, and Severe Dysphagia in a Young Girl With 14q22q23 Microdeletion Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: 14q22q23 microdeletion syndrome, also called Frias syndrome, is an extremely rare partial deletion of the long arm of chromosome 14 characterized by the anomalies of the pituitary gland, eyes, and hand/foot.

evidence.reference_title: Interstitial deletion 14q22.3-q23.2: genotype-phenotype correlation.
evidence.snippet: Here, we present a 12-year-old girl with dysmorphic face, choanal atresia, gastroesophageal reflux, and moderate developmental delay, in whom an interstitial deletion 14q22.3-q23.2 was detected using a 180k array comparative genome hybridization.

nodes.description: Dosage loss of OTX2 and neighboring SIX-family genes disrupts ocular, pituitary, hypothalamic, facial-bone, and brain developmental programs.
nodes.id: OTX2 and SIX-region ocular-pituitary developmental disruption

biological_processes: regulation of transcription by RNA polymerase II

evidence.reference_title: Interstitial deletion 14q22.3-q23.2: genotype-phenotype correlation.
evidence.snippet: The 6.5 Mb deletion contains 27 genes, including three genes of the SIX family: SIX1, SIX4, and SIX6.

evidence.reference_title: Interstitial deletion 14q22.3-q23.2: genotype-phenotype correlation.
evidence.snippet: In mammals, Six1 has been shown to be involved in ocular differentiation, whereas Six4 and Six6 are primarily expressed in the hypothalamus, pituitary gland, and facial bones.

evidence.reference_title: New insights into the phenotypic spectrum of 14q22q23 deletions: a case report and literature review.
evidence.snippet: In this report, we describe a case of large microdeletion encompassing OTX2 but not BMP4 presenting with a syndromic anophthalmia with corpus callosum hypoplasia, pituitary gland hypoplasia and vermian hypoplasia.

nodes.description: Pituitary hypoplasia is a core endocrine manifestation of the deletion syndrome. The local HPO cache does not expose a separate general "pituitary gland hypoplasia" term, so this entry uses anterior pituitary hypoplasia as the closest phenotype-level HPO mapping.

evidence.reference_title: New insights into the phenotypic spectrum of 14q22q23 deletions: a case report and literature review.
evidence.snippet: In this report, we describe a case of large microdeletion encompassing OTX2 but not BMP4 presenting with a syndromic anophthalmia with corpus callosum hypoplasia, pituitary gland hypoplasia and vermian hypoplasia.

nodes.description: Supernumerary digits may occur as part of the hand/foot anomaly spectrum.

evidence.reference_title: Haploinsufficiency of BMP4 and OTX2 in the Foetus with an abnormal facial profile detected in the first trimester of pregnancy.
evidence.snippet: Interstitial microdeletion 14q22q23 is a rare chromosomal syndrome associated with variable defects: microphthalmia/anophthalmia, pituitary anomalies, polydactyly/syndactyly of hands and feet, micrognathia/retrognathia.

nodes.description: Short stature is part of the extra-ocular phenotype associated with OTX2 disruption.

evidence.reference_title: New insights into the phenotypic spectrum of 14q22q23 deletions: a case report and literature review.
evidence.snippet: Mutations occurring in the orthodenticle homeobox 2 gene (OTX2) are responsible for a rare genetic syndrome, characterized mainly by microphthalmia/anophthalmia associated with extra-ocular defects such as brain malformations, pituitary abnormalities, short stature and intellectual disability.

nodes.description: Webbing or fusion of digits can accompany polydactyly in the hand/foot anomaly spectrum.

evidence.reference_title: Haploinsufficiency of BMP4 and OTX2 in the Foetus with an abnormal facial profile detected in the first trimester of pregnancy.
evidence.snippet: Interstitial microdeletion 14q22q23 is a rare chromosomal syndrome associated with variable defects: microphthalmia/anophthalmia, pituitary anomalies, polydactyly/syndactyly of hands and feet, micrognathia/retrognathia.
